mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are negative in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS is below 1.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 6.5%, labor market slack suppresses wage growth and consumer spending expectations, causing immediate negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Positive 5-day returns occur in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts signals broad-based domestic demand recovery, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates strengthening manufacturing capacity utilization, which translates to higher corporate profit margins and positive equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive returns during early-cycle expansions, or the signal reverses direction when capacity utilization exceeds 80%.

mechanism_narrative: Steady growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, generating positive short-term S&P 500 momentum over the next week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >55% of months with positive payroll growth, or the correlation between payroll changes and returns drops below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 236.5, inflationary pressures force expectations of tighter monetary policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Returns remain positive in >60% of threshold-crossing events, or the predictive power vanishes when core inflation remains below 2.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and real personal consumption expenditures confirms sustainable aggregate demand expansion, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative returns in >50% of observations, or the interaction effect is indistinguishable from zero in a panel regression.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals manufacturing contraction and impending earnings downgrades, triggering negative short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: Negative returns occur in <40% of late-cycle industrial production declines, or the signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark in walk-forward tests.
